assets: ability to define structured drafting and evidence processes
network_access: a few warm fintech and compliance contacts
constraints: must avoid unauthorized legal or regulated-role representation
must keep humans in the loop for substantive judgments
must not use confidential prior employer or client materials without rights
credibility_signals: direct prior experience supporting compliance teams at a fintech
unfair_advantages: understands the real workflow behind regulator responses better than generic AI builders
can position a narrow painful workflow instead of a vague AI compliance promise

binding_constraints: Do not market or operate as a replacement compliance officer, MLRO, or legal advisor.
All substantive outputs require client review and approval before use or submission.
Keep the service on drafting, evidence gathering, workflow management, and exam-readiness operations.
Escalate legal interpretation, licensing questions, and formal sign-off to the client's designated professional.

opportunities.title: AI regulator-response prep concierge for small fintechs
opportunities.thesis: Sell a productized service that converts messy internal facts into draft response packs, evidence requests, and reviewer-ready first drafts for client approval.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has direct domain experience with regulator-response workflows and better buyer empathy than generic automation consultants.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The work may be too bespoke or too tightly tied to outside counsel unless the offer is narrowly scoped.
validation_plan: Pitch 12 warm or warm-adjacent prospects within 7 days.
Book 4 discovery calls and show one synthetic demo pack.
Ask for a paid pilot in the $2k-$5k range.
kill_criteria: Fewer than 2 prospects show willingness to pay for a scoped pilot.
Buyers consistently expect officer-level sign-off rather than workflow support.

opportunities.title: Compliance documentation and controls evidence pack service
opportunities.thesis: Help small regulated firms maintain audit trails, policy-to-control mappings, issue logs, and exam-readiness document rooms.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This is adjacent to the user's lived experience and easier to keep on the operations side of the regulated boundary.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Demand may feel less urgent than live regulator-response work unless tied to an exam or remediation trigger.
validation_plan: Test demand on the same 4 discovery calls.
Offer a fixed-scope readiness package to 2 prospects.
Measure whether urgency is lower than response-prep demand.
kill_criteria: Prospects treat this as nice-to-have rather than budgeted pain.
The service becomes too bespoke to standardize.

opportunities.title: Regulator response workspace for small regulated firms
opportunities.thesis: Build a narrow SaaS that structures regulator requests, tracks evidence, drafts first-pass responses, and enforces human review.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Service work can uncover the common workflow and data model before software build-out.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Software-first is too early if request types and buyer urgency are not yet standardized.
validation_plan: Confirm at least 60% workflow overlap across engagements.
Validate that 2 clients want ongoing tooling.
kill_criteria: Less than 40% workflow reuse across customers.
No buyer requests ongoing software after paid service work.

opportunities.title: Response prep concierge to compliance workflow product
opportunities.thesis: Start with a tightly scoped regulator-response preparation service, then productize recurring intake, evidence collection, drafting, escalation, and review workflow into a lightweight compliance operations platform.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This path uses the user's domain edge immediately, monetizes through warm contacts, and collects process insight without overclaiming regulated authority.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: If the first engagements are too custom or too infrequent, there may be no repeatable product wedge.
validation_plan: Launch a one-page offer and contact 12 prospects within 7 days.
Book 4 discovery calls and get 2 paid pilot yeses or serious procurement discussions.
Map the workflow into 6-10 standard steps and confirm at least 60% reuse.
Schedule 1 partnership conversation with a compliance advisor or law firm for edge cases.
kill_criteria: Fewer than 4 of 12 outreach messages get replies in 7 days.
Fewer than 2 of 4 calls produce willingness to pay for a pilot.
Interested buyers want delegated officer-level responsibility rather than support tooling.
Less than 40% of workflow steps are reusable across prospects.

rejected_paths.title: Lightweight outsourced compliance officer for small firms
rejected_paths.reason: This positioning implies delegated regulated authority and ongoing professional judgment, creating legal and compliance overreach risk.

rejected_paths.title: Software-first AI compliance copilot
rejected_paths.reason: Too early without paid validation and a clear repeatable workflow across multiple firms.

next_actions: Draft a one-page offer for a 48-hour regulator response prep sprint with explicit human-review language.
Send 12 warm outreach messages within 7 days.
Run 4 discovery calls and show a synthetic demo pack.
Ask for 2 paid pilots priced at $2k-$5k.
Find 1 compliance advisor or law firm partner for substantive edge cases.
